last_updated_note: Phase B step 1 — appended COMM-2026-04-29-016 for first unified ledger Box (temporal_continuity)
core_rule: If a future agent needs to hear it, leave it where they can find it.

active_entries.title: Ledger Build Rhythm
active_entries.promote_when: rhythm changes (e.g., parallelization is approved)
active_entries.message: Build ledger outlines and implementations one at a time. The user types P (or sends the next ledger spec as an upload) to proceed to the next ledger.
active_entries.suggested_action: Continue ledger work in order. Do not skip ahead. Do not batch.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review after first wave of ledgers is implemented (likely Phase 8-10).
active_entries.archive_after: one full session cycle per Communications Ledger Section 12

active_entries.title: Client Box Cleanup Rhythm
active_entries.promote_when: client-box audit pattern formalizes (Decisions Ledger candidate)
active_entries.message: Client Box audits should happen one box at a time. Read the box (including 01b_comms_verbatim.md), report findings, clean if approved, leave an audit marker, then proceed.
active_entries.suggested_action: For each box, check comms, state, profile, seven-day plan, alerts, ledger, guardrails, calendar drift, risky commitments, enrichment boundary, and plan validity. Brenda & Steve is the template.
active_entries.expiry_review: Downgrade when all 28 active Client Boxes have been audited.

active_entries.title: Brenda & Steve Fee-Waiver Lesson
active_entries.promote_when: approval-card workflow exists -> promote to Decisions
active_entries.message: The Brenda & Steve fee-waiver move may have been strategically correct, but it was too risky to be hidden inside a rushed batch approval flow. Future risky moves should be isolated and explicitly approved.
active_entries.suggested_action: When a plan contains fee waivers, no-charge language, discounts, guest-count promises, pricing commitments, or enrichment-based personalization, convert it into an approval-required card or flatten the copy.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review after approval-card workflow exists in the automation page.
active_entries.archive_after: one full session cycle per Communications Ledger Section 12

active_entries.title: Cleanup Phase, Not Greenfield (Build Arc Context)
active_entries.from: User (Jake) via Claude Code catch-up session
active_entries.promote_when: cleanup phase ends -> Decisions Ledger entry naming the next phase
active_entries.message: The project is 3 days in, in cleanup phase, not greenfield. Build arc: plumbing-first attempt was scrapped under Rodrigo pressure -> UI rebuilt from working pieces -> automations/Boxes/Onboard Scripts authored on a heroic day, now symlinked into bedrock at Auto/ -> Ledger Phases 1-6 + first steward live. Now: cleanup. Bedrock needs triad reconciliation (PROB-016, deferred).
active_entries.suggested_action: Read this before suggesting structural changes. Defer to PROB-016 for bedrock work. Preserve Auto/ as read-mostly. Don't force greenfield patterns onto a cleanup project.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review when Phase B (sub-agents) and Phase C (Subagent Boxes) complete and bedrock reconciliation can run safely.

active_entries.title: Three-Phase Build Discipline (A -> B -> C)
active_entries.promote_when: phase order is settled in Decisions Ledger
active_entries.message: Phase A: finish all Ledgers (current). Phase B: finish all Sub-agents (graduate Subagent Boxes packages into runnable app agents). Phase C: build Subagent Boxes (boxes of ledgers controlled by sub-agent configurations). Do not skip ahead. Do not parallelize without explicit approval.
active_entries.suggested_action: When asked to build something with state, confirm which phase the project is in before proposing structure.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review on Phase A completion.
active_entries.archive_after: one full session cycle per Communications Ledger Section 12

active_entries.title: Watch For Local Git Drift
active_entries.promote_when: stable branch workflow is adopted -> archive
active_entries.message: The user may have local unpushed work while agents are writing directly to GitHub. The working tree is currently dirty with multiple sessions' uncommitted edits.
active_entries.suggested_action: Run git status before any direct GitHub write. Never force-push main. Local commits are fine if explicitly requested; pushing is Jake's call.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive once stable branch workflow is adopted and git status is consistently clean before agent writes.

active_entries.title: Plans May Predate Guardrails
active_entries.promote_when: all 28 boxes audited -> downgrade to lesson, then archive
active_entries.message: Some seven-day plans were written before current inbox guardrails were fully enforced. They may contain stale dates, risky commitment language, enrichment leakage, or unsafe send assumptions.
active_entries.suggested_action: Before trusting any plan, audit it against current comms (including 01b_comms_verbatim.md), state, and guardrails. Reply received -> plan is stale.
active_entries.expiry_review: Downgrade once all 28 active Client Boxes carry an Audit-cleaned line and an audit marker.

active_entries.title: Bedrock Reconciliation Deferred (See PROB-016)
active_entries.promote_when: ledger + sub-agent buildouts complete -> execute reconciliation, then archive
active_entries.message: CCAgentindex/ was bootstrapped on the fly before the triad philosophy was formalized. The 32-subdir layout needs triad-based reconciliation, but do not act on this yet. Reconciliation is blocked on Phase A and Phase B completion.
active_entries.suggested_action: If you notice bedrock-shaped issues, add evidence to PROB-016, not to this ledger. Communications carries the pointer; Open Problems owns the tracking.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when PROB-016 closes.

active_entries.title: Preferred Ledger Writing Style
active_entries.promote_when: style guide solidifies -> Decisions Ledger entry
active_entries.message: User prefers ledger writeups that are broad, gorgeous, implementation-ready, and high-level enough that agents do not overfit to a single implementation. Outline drafts at /Ledger Drafts/ are meant to be handed to Opus for implementation.
active_entries.suggested_action: Keep ledger outlines elegant, practical, and implementation-oriented. Avoid narrow overengineering unless asked. Read the matching outline at /Ledger Drafts/<name>.txt first.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review when style guide is formalized in a Decisions Ledger entry.

active_entries.title: Safe Movement Preference
active_entries.promote_when: trust threshold is named in Decisions Ledger
active_entries.message: Comeketo (Rodrigo) wants to see automation movement, but Jake wants the early system to be careful. Prefer safe movement over aggressive cleverness while trust is being established.
active_entries.suggested_action: Flatten risky plans unless the risky move is explicitly approved. Use polite nudges, clear next-step asks, reply-aware handling, and guardrail checks.
active_entries.expiry_review: Review when explicit trust thresholds are named.

active_entries.title: Triad Spine: Box + Ledger + Sub-agent
active_entries.from: User (Jake) via Claude Code catch-up session
active_entries.promote_when: Decisions Ledger lands -> promote as a settled architecture decision
active_entries.message: Architectural spine is the Box + Ledger + Sub-agent triad. Anything stateful gets all three. Box = unit of state. Ledger = legible memory. Sub-agent = operator. Standing rule: legibility is more important than the build itself. Code without a corresponding Ledger entry is debt to repay.
active_entries.suggested_action: Before designing anything stateful, ask: where is the Box? where is the Ledger? where will the Sub-agent live? If any answer is 'we'll figure that out later', stop and figure it out now.
active_entries.expiry_review: Promote to Decisions Ledger when that ledger lands. Currently a Communication only because Decisions doesn't exist yet — but the rule itself is settled.
active_entries.archive_after: one full session cycle per Communications Ledger Section 12

active_entries.title: Plumbing-First Build Attempt
active_entries.promote_when: n/a (historical context only)
active_entries.what_was_attempted: First build pass on Comeketo Agent was plumbing-first: build out ledger system and sub-agent scaffolding before user-facing UI matured.
active_entries.result: Spine work was real, but Rodrigo (Comeketo owner) needed something usable/demoable sooner than the plumbing-first plan permitted.
active_entries.why_stopped: Pressure to demo collided with the plumbing timeline. Jake scrapped the plumbing-first plan mid-stream and pivoted: rebuilt UI from working pieces, then on a heroic day via Claude Co-Work authored almost all automations/Boxes/Onboard Scripts in a separate folder (now symlinked into bedrock at Auto/).
future_agents_should_know: Bedrock layout (CCAgentindex/) carries the bootstrapped-era 32-subdir shape from this attempt. PROB-016 owns the eventual reconciliation.
Six bedrock subdirs are aliases into Auto/: Boxes, Client Boxes, Staff Boxes, comeketo-inbox, Onboard Scripts, orchestrator. This was the salvage path from the pivot.
The 5 draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ are surviving plumbing-first work. They are the Phase B starting point — not greenfield.
Lesson is not 'plumbing-first is wrong.' Lesson is that owner pressure can collide with depth-first plans, and the rebuild should preserve as much of the prior spine as possible.

active_entries.title: Definition of Done Is Now The Closing Authority
active_entries.from: Cowork session (Phase 9 ledger build, autonomous next-pick under Jake's 'you make the call' delegation)
active_entries.to: Future agents — every Phase A ledger build, every Phase B sub-agent graduation, every Page Ledger pass.
active_entries.promote_when: a steward agent for DoD is built (Phase B), at which point this entry archives — the steward enforces the gate automatically
active_entries.summary: Definition of Done Ledger is now live. Universal Done Gate (8 questions) + 11 work-type sub-gates + Ledger Update Matrix. Page-asset sitemap remains canonical UI Done Gate (DoD §5.3 elevates it, does not replace it). Every new ledger must satisfy DoD §5.6. Box Ledger registration gap (Phase 8 landed earlier today but not yet indexed) was fixed in the same sprint.
active_entries.suggested_action: Before closing any meaningful task: walk DoD §4 Universal Done Gate plus the matching §5 sub-gate. When authoring a new ledger: read DoD §5.6 first, use §6 Ledger Update Matrix as the fastest 'what do I touch?' reference. When building a Phase B steward: the steward's job is to enforce its ledger's Done Gate plus DoD §6 cross-ledger updates.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when DoD steward is built (Phase B). Review §5 if a new work-type Done Gate is needed (e.g., Subagent Box Done Gate when Phase C starts).

active_entries.title: Box Bus Ledger Landed — Schema-Only Architectural Lock
active_entries.from: Cowork session (Phase 10 ledger build, with explicit pushback against deferring the architecture)
active_entries.to: Future agents — every Phase 11 ledger build, every new Box authored, every Phase B sub-agent graduation, every Phase C runtime contributor.
affected_systems: every stateful entity in the project
every ledger authored from this point forward
every Box authored from this point forward
active_entries.promote_when: Phase C runtime ships and the bus is consuming envelopes — at that point this entry archives because the running system enforces what this entry asks for.
active_entries.summary: Box Bus Ledger is now live. SCHEMA ONLY — no router, no propagation ledger, no interpreters, no validation runtime. DEC-2026-04-29-013 locks the architecture and DEC-2026-04-29-008's runtime deferral still binds. What this gives every future contributor: a written target for manifest shape (box.json), envelope shape, routing tiers (Global/Domain/Local), interpreter tiers (T1/T2/T3), cycle policy (hard refusal), and 'what's a Box / what's not' rule. Two worked examples ship with the ledger: Hugo Casillas (Client Box) and Analytics Source Channels Snapshot Box. Box Ledger now points at Bus Ledger for the manifest schema; DoD §5.7 added a manifest-stub check (soft today, hard at Phase C).
active_entries.suggested_action: When authoring a new ledger from Phase 11 onward: declare its tier (global/domain/local) and entry kind aligned to the envelope schema in BOX_BUS_LEDGER.md §2.2. When stamping a new Box: ship a box.json manifest stub even if subscribes[] and emits[] start empty. When building Phase B steward agents: include manifest authoring for the steward's target ledger as part of graduation. DO NOT ship runtime (router, propagation ledger, interpreters, validation enforcement) during Phase A. If runtime feels tempting, route the urge into Open Problems Ledger or Communications Ledger — not into code.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when Phase C runtime ships and validates the schema in production. Review fields if a foreseen-but-unspecified manifest field is needed (e.g., during real Box authoring it turns out subscribes[] needs a 'priority' field) — that's a BOX_BUS_LEDGER schema update + possibly a new Decision.

active_entries.title: Source-of-Truth Ledger Landed — First Envelope-Aware Ledger
active_entries.to: Future agents — every customer-facing send, every Client Box audit, every Phase B steward graduation, every Phase C runtime contributor.
affected_systems: Boxes UI page (renderer can now honor allowed_to_use)
the eventual bus runtime — manifests' source_of_truth fields validate against §3 trust orderings
related_ledgers: GLOBAL_LEDGER.md §4 (now points at SOURCE_OF_TRUTH as canonical home)
active_entries.promote_when: Allowed-To-Know schema gets implemented in at least 3 Client Boxes — at that point this entry archives because the implementation provides its own audit trail.
active_entries.summary: Source-of-Truth Ledger is now live. Consolidated rules from Global §4, NS-06, FDL §6, DoD §5.5 into one canonical home. Universal trust ordering + per-domain orderings (Client / Page / Project state / Settled rules / External system / Generated-vs-canonical / Plans-vs-comms). Allowed-To-Know four-bucket schema (comms_confirmed / internal_strategy / protected / approval_required) with per-Box implementation contract — this closes the schema-design close-criterion of PROB-2026-04-28-001 (status open→partial). First ledger authored under DEC-2026-04-29-013 envelope-aware rule: declares tier=global, cross-references BOX_BUS_LEDGER §2.1 for manifest source_of_truth field semantics.
active_entries.suggested_action: Before authoring customer-facing copy: walk SOURCE_OF_TRUTH §3.1 client trust ordering + §4 allowed-to-know schema. When stamping a new Client Box: include allowed_to_use.json stub per §4.2 contract (start with empty buckets — populate as facts emerge). When source-of-truth ownership shifts: walk §6 update protocol (DoD §5.5 Done Gate). When two surfaces disagree: walk §5 conflict resolution — never silently merge. Hugo Casillas is the natural test case for first allowed_to_use.json implementation (most enrichment, oldest active cadence).
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when allowed_to_use.json is implemented in at least 3 Client Boxes and one read-path honors it. Review §3 trust orderings if a new domain emerges (e.g., when Connections Ledger lands, external systems get their own per-service orderings).

active_entries.summary: Connections Ledger is now live. First DOMAIN-tier ledger (proves envelope-aware authoring works beyond global). 14 services classified active + 2 planned + 3 not-in-use, every classification grep-evidenced against server.py + .env + connectors.js + docs/connectors.md. Twilio (which the draft outline missed) is registered with full WhatsApp + SMS credential map. Slack token-format issue (xapp- vs xoxb-) is flagged as an open problem candidate. PROB-2026-04-28-009 (optional connections need verification) is CLOSED. No raw secrets in the ledger — only env var names and locations.
active_entries.suggested_action: Before adding a new service: read CONNECTIONS §10 Done Gate first. Before declaring a Box subscribes[] entry: confirm the service is active (not planned/not-in-use/needs-verification). When a service starts misbehaving: walk CONNECTIONS §6 Failure Mode Map. When rotating a credential: update the env var name only if it changed; never the value.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when Settings Ledger lands. Review §4 service registry quarterly to catch drift.

active_entries.title: File Contents Ledger Landed — ~78 File Entries

active_entries.summary: File Contents Ledger is now live. ~78 load-bearing files across 10 categories. Each entry: purpose (one paragraph), owns, type label (canonical/generated/template/etc), edit guidance, dependencies, companion-file rules, common mistakes, verification, related ledgers/decisions. Categories: 13 ledgers, 17 runtime-frontend, 1 runtime-backend, 8 meta-harness, 11 orchestrator, 5 inbox-skill, 4 bedrock-index, 16 client-box-convention, 1 staff-box-convention, 2 templates. Tier: global. Cross-references SoT trust orderings (per-Box file ranks 1-9), DoD §5.5 + §5.7 Done Gates, Connections (services → which files use them), Decisions (file-level rules), Open Problems (file-level issues like PROB-2026-04-28-010).
active_entries.suggested_action: Before editing an unfamiliar file: check FILE_CONTENTS for its entry. When adding a new file that meets §2 inclusion rule: add an entry. When promoting/demoting a file (canonical ↔ generated): trigger SoT §6 update protocol AND update its entry here. Default to NOT adding entries for utility/leaf files — the inclusion rule exists to keep signal high.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when steward agent is built and starts auditing drift instead of seeding.

When you touch lucide.js / styles.css / Secretary.html: bump cache-busters per DEC-2026-04-29-007.

active_entries.to: Future agents — every Settings page change, every new credential editor, every new feature flag.

active_entries.promote_when: Settings steward is built — at that point the audit log is auto-checked and this entry archives.
active_entries.summary: Settings Ledger is now live. 11 user-changeable settings cataloged across 3 persistence layers (.env server-side / localStorage.secretary.tweaks client-side / runtime-only). Documented: theme, language, demo mode, intelligence provider (3-way exclusive: claude_code/openai/codex_cli), OpenAI model, Pieces model, prompt enhancement, browser-use defaults (4 sub-fields), MCP credential editor (allowlisted save/clear with audit log to activity.jsonl, key name only never value), MCP server status registry (read-only from /api/status), reset. Provider exclusivity rule + demo mode rule + credential save/clear flow + audit rule all written down. Tier: global. Pairs cleanly with Connections (services) and Asset/Widget Map (localStorage).
active_entries.suggested_action: Before adding a new toggle to SettingsScreen: walk SETTINGS §3 to pick the right persistence layer. Before adding a new credential editor: 1) update server.py allowlist, 2) add UI editor row, 3) update CONNECTIONS.md, 4) update SETTINGS §3.8 + §4.1. Demo mode is the safest send-blocker — every customer-facing automation should respect it. Provider switching is instant; no restart needed; the picker writes to tweaks.aiProvider and routes self-select. NEVER log a raw secret, anywhere. The save flow is the only path to .env.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when Settings steward is built and starts auditing the .env allowlist + localStorage shape against this ledger automatically.

active_entries.title: First Page Ledger Landed (boxes) — Per-Page Deep-Memory Pattern Established
active_entries.from: Cowork session (Phase 16, Jake's call: 'Let's hit the Page Ledger')
active_entries.to: Future agents — every page change beyond cosmetic; every operator-onboarding session; every Phase B sub-agent that audits per-page state.

page_asset_sitemap.md (sitemap remains canonical; Page Ledgers are the narrative layer)

active_entries.promote_when: Every high-risk page has a Page Ledger (intake, automation, delegations, settings) — at that point this entry archives because the pattern is established and self-replicating.
active_entries.summary: First Page Ledger landed: LEDGERS/PAGES/boxes.md. 16 substantive sections. Tier: domain. Sitemap §boxes remains canonical operational truth (DEC-2026-04-29-007); Page Ledger carries the WHY — why Hugo and Brenda have specialized renderers and the other 26 don't, why dossier rendering moved from markdown-dump to design-kit narrative cards, why completeness scoring landed when it did, where the page is heading through Phase C runtime. Establishes the per-page deep-memory pattern. LEDGERS/PAGES/_README.md stamps the directory with the pattern + authoring template + status of all 14 routes (1 active, 13 planned). Anti-pattern explicitly named: don't author all 14 at once; better to land one well-grounded Page Ledger than 14 thin stubs.
active_entries.suggested_action: When the next Page Ledger is needed: read LEDGERS/PAGES/_README.md for the authoring template + first-wave priority list. When changing the boxes page: walk DoD §5.3 + LEDGERS/PAGES/boxes.md §10 (local-stricter Done Gate). When sitemap and Page Ledger disagree on per-page operational detail: SITEMAP WINS. Page Ledger is the narrative layer; sitemap is the operational truth.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when high-risk first wave (intake / automation / delegations / settings) all have Page Ledgers — pattern is then self-sustaining.

active_entries.summary: Second Page Ledger landed: LEDGERS/PAGES/intake.md. 16 substantive sections. Tier: domain. Risk: medium-high. Captures the Phase 1 unification (DEC-005..008) architecture: Box Reports synthesized over Auto/Client Boxes/<Name>/, refresh on read, slug=box_id, ask path stays generic in Phase 1. Documents: writes-table split (Box Report vs Workspace behavior matrices for every endpoint), 5 page-specific guardrails (allowed-to-know discipline, ingest-not-canonical, delegations-as-highest-risk, identity-uniqueness, refresh-on-read awareness), 5 open problems including the deferred Phase 2 / Phase 3 work, and 6 architectural-rationale entries explaining the Reports list split, synthesized-not-stored, slug=box_id, ask-stays-generic, Box-folder writes, and right-click-density choices.

honest_accounting_for_jake.where_we_are: 15/15 project-level ledgers + 2/14 Page Ledgers. System is structurally complete.
honest_accounting_for_jake.what_is_load_bearing_remaining: 3 more high-risk Page Ledgers (automation, delegations, settings page) + Phase Ledger = ~4 more substantive turns to Phase A done
honest_accounting_for_jake.what_is_defer_or_skip: 9 lower-risk Page Ledgers (grid, leads, clients, coworkers, contacts, venues, briefing, activity, analytics), Widget Ledgers, Prompt/Reconstruction, Scout — defer until needed
honest_accounting_for_jake.fastest_cut_option: stop after intake + Phase Ledger (1 more turn) = Phase A done

active_entries.title: Phase A Complete — Close-Out + Phase B Handoff
active_entries.from: Cowork session (final propagation pass; Jake's call: 'let's just bang it all out')
active_entries.to: Future agents — every Phase B contributor; every cold-starting session expecting Phase A in flight (it's not anymore).
affected_systems: every active session expecting Phase A in flight
the 5 draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ (now eligible for graduation)
Phase C runtime work (still deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013)

active_entries.promote_when: Phase B has its first runnable steward graduation — at that point this entry archives because the next phase is in flight.
active_entries.summary: Phase A is complete as of 2026-04-29. 17 active ledgers across global + domain tiers. Every architectural pattern future work depends on is named, written down, cross-referenced. High-risk Page Ledger wave landed (boxes, intake, automation, delegations, settings page) — captures the WHY behind each high-risk surface. Phase Ledger (PHASE.md) closes Phase A and names Phase B do-not-leak rules. DEC-2026-04-29-014 declares Phase A complete and unblocks Phase B (Sub-agents). Phase C runtime stays deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013. The 9 lower-risk Page Ledgers + Widget Ledgers + Prompt/Reconstruction + Scout + per-directory Box.md stamps are explicitly deferred — author when needed during Phase B/C, not as Phase A reopens.

honest_accounting_close_out.decisions_recorded: 14 active (8 from Apr 28 + 6 from Apr 29 sprint)
honest_accounting_close_out.next_phase: Phase B — Sub-agents (5 draft packages at /Subagent Boxes/ eligible for graduation)

active_entries.suggested_action: Before any Phase B work: read PHASE.md §4 (Phase B scope + exit criteria + do-not-leak rules) AND DEC-2026-04-29-014 (Phase A Complete). When graduating a draft sub-agent package: copy the global_ledger_steward pattern (canonical config in LEDGERS/AGENTS/<name>/, runnable form at CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/, run receipts at CCAgentindex/_ledger/<name>_runs/). When Phase B feels too easy to graduate quickly into Phase C: STOP. Phase C runtime stays deferred per DEC-2026-04-29-013. The phasing rule is the protection.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when Phase B has at least 3 graduated stewards running. At that point this close-out is no longer the current handoff.

active_entries.title: First Unified Ledger Box Landed — temporal_continuity (Phase B Step 1)
active_entries.from: Cowork session (Phase B step 1; Jake's call: 'compile these together so that ledgers and boxes and sub-agents are all one thing')
active_entries.to: Future agents — every Phase B steward graduation; every operator looking for a steward; every Phase C runtime contributor.
affected_systems: Phase B target structure (refined from CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ to LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/)
the 5 draft packages at /Subagent Boxes/ (now have a clear graduation path)
LEDGERS/AGENTS/global_ledger_steward/ (existing canonical — queued for reorg into LEDGERS/BOXES/global_ledger/steward/)
BOX_LEDGER.md and BOX_BUS_LEDGER.md (this Box is the first real instance of their patterns)
related_ledgers: DECISIONS_LEDGER.md DEC-2026-04-29-001 (Triad Spine — this Box realizes structural side)
DECISIONS_LEDGER.md DEC-2026-04-29-013 (Reactive Box Network runtime deferred — this Box doesn't violate runtime deferral)
DECISIONS_LEDGER.md DEC-2026-04-29-014 (Phase A Complete — first Phase B work)
PHASE.md §4 (Phase B target — needs update to reflect new layout)
active_entries.promote_when: All 5 draft packages at /Subagent Boxes/ have been graduated into LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/ — at that point this entry archives because the pattern is established and self-replicating.
active_entries.summary: First unified ledger Box landed: LEDGERS/BOXES/temporal_continuity/. Files: box.json (manifest per BOX_BUS §2.1) + BOX.md (per-Box orientation) + steward/{AGENTS.md, config.json, prompt.md} + receipts/. Ledger files (TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md + .json) STAY at LEDGERS/ — owned by the Box via path reference, never moved. Pattern locked by DEC-2026-04-29-015. Refines Phase B target: instead of 'graduate to CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/', the new target is 'graduate to LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/' — co-located with the manifest, the per-Box orientation, and the receipts dir. The runnable form (POST /api/agents/temporal_continuity_steward/run) is NOT yet wired — that's the next Phase B step. The structural unification landed first.
active_entries.suggested_action: Next Phase B steps in priority order: (1) Reorganize LEDGERS/AGENTS/global_ledger_steward/ → LEDGERS/BOXES/global_ledger/steward/ (move + author box.json + BOX.md). (2) Graduate the remaining 3 drafts at /Subagent Boxes/ (file_directory, north_star, open_problems) into LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/. (3) Author Boxes for the ledgers without drafts (communications, decisions, dod, sot, connections, file_contents, asset_widget_map, settings, box, box_bus, phase). (4) Wire each steward's runnable form (decide whether CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/ stays or consolidates with LEDGERS/BOXES/<name>/steward/). DO NOT move TEMPORAL_CONTINUITY.md or any other ledger files into the Box folders — that breaks the Read-First protocol per DEC-2026-04-29-015 do-not-undo-casually.
active_entries.expiry_review: Archive when 16 ledger Boxes are stamped and at least 5 stewards are runnable. At that point Phase B is in late stages and this close-out is no longer the current handoff.

This Communications entry

lessons_learned.title: Sitting There Is State
lessons_learned.summary: Silence, waiting, holding, and watching are not absence. They are states. Future systems should represent waiting states explicitly.

lessons_learned.title: Automation Can Become More Personal
lessons_learned.summary: The goal is not generic drip automation. The goal is automation that remembers what people said, respects their context, and helps Andre show up prepared. Wholesome enrichment is allowed for internal understanding. Creepy personalization is not.

lessons_learned.title: Wholesome Enrichment vs Creepy Personalization
lessons_learned.summary: Enrichment is allowed for internal strategy. It is not customer-facing. Customer-facing copy must use only facts found in comms or with explicit operator approval.
allowed_sources_for_customer_copy: explicit operator approval for one-off uses

lessons_learned.title: TCL/GL Drift Is The Failure Mode
lessons_learned.summary: Cold-starting agents read TCL and Global Ledger first. If those ledgers are stale, every agent that boots from them is stale. The fix is discipline: whoever does the work also updates the orientation surface in the same unit of work.
lessons_learned.jake_exact_words: The ledger is more important than the actual work. Literally everything needs to be ledgered.
lessons_learned.suggested_action: Treat TCL and Global Ledger updates as part of the change, not an optional epilogue. Update header dates. Append to Section 3 Recent Meaningful Changes. Update Section 1 Current Snapshot if anything structural changed.
lessons_learned.expiry_review: Does not expire. This is the meta-rule that keeps every other ledger usable.
lessons_learned.archive_after: one full session cycle per Communications Ledger Section 12

cross_system_notes.title: Client Box State Flow
cross_system_notes.summary: Client comms feed state. State affects plan validity. Plan validity affects scheduled fires. Scheduled fires must obey guardrails. Boxes UI displays state but does not own it.

cross_system_notes.title: Page Work Done Gate
cross_system_notes.summary: Any page, route, or page data-binding change must update page_asset_sitemap.md in the same unit of work. UI Done Gate.

archive_when: lesson became a North Star principle
archive_policy.archive_format: leave entry in place with status='promoted' or 'archived' and a closing line: 'Archived: YYYY-MM-DD - <reason>'
